content_body:
Enjoying a cabin trip to Blue Ridge, GA is one of the most beautiful experiences you can have in the North Georgia area, even on rainy days. While Blue Ridge is known for its outdoor adventures and the nature surrounding it, there are a wide variety of things that you can do rain or shine. With the rain comes an often-overlooked charm of the Blue Ridge mountains, the beauty of the people and unique activities that will keep you entertained during the rain.

Fun Activities in Downtown Blue Ridge

Blue Ridge Scenic Railway  

The Blue Ridge Scenic Railway offers a tour of the amazing scenery and picturesque views in the Blue Ridge area. The Toccoa River, the small town of McCaysville, mountainside cabins, and other historic sites in the Blue Ridge Mountains can be seen in this 26-mile roundtrip journey with a 2-hour layover in McCaysville,GA/Copperhill, TN where you can stand in two states at one time. The rain adds to the scenery and offers a peaceful and relaxing atmosphere while sipping on some coffee and enjoying the train ride.

Breweries  

Blue Ridge is no stranger to high quality craft beer, and with the many amazing breweries in the Blue Ridge area, you won’t be either! With our well-known breweries such as Grumpy Old Men Brewing, Fannin Fannin Brewing Company, or Angry Hops, you will never be short of amazing variety, taste, and experience! After all, who doesn’t like to enjoy awesome hangouts with locals and good beer in the rain?

Vertigo Pinball

 Want a blast from the past to wait out the weather on Main Street Downtown Blue Ridge? Look no further than Vertigo Pinball! Featuring many old and new pinball machines, some of which being very rare and hard to come by, Vertigo Pinball offers a ton of fun for families, friends, and couples! Pinball, food, and beer, what more could you ask for on a rainy day?

2nd Story Entertainment  

Sporting an indoor family-fun center on Main Street featuring a full 18-hole mini-gold course weaving in and out of 8 individually themed rooms of a 112-year-old hotel, 2nd Story Entertainment can boast an amazing time for any group of any age range! They also feature arcade rooms, an outside deck, giant Connect 4, giant checkers, and magnetic darts! At 2nd Story Entertainment you will find plenty of things to do while waiting for the rain to stop!

Mountain Man Comics 

Selling raw and slabbed comics, trade paperbacks, graphic novels, manga, statues, and related collectibles from the Golden Age, Silver age, Modern Age, thru the present, spend a rainy afternoon browsing through all the cool comics on Main Street

Escape Blue Ridge

Experience the thrill of Escape Blue Ridge, located in Downtown Blue Ridge. An escape game in which players are "locked" in a themed room, and have to use elements of the room to solve a series of puzzles and escape within a set time limit. Great for team building, family bonding, or just a blast with a group of friends, this mental thrill is the top of our list of things to do on a rainy day in Blue Ridge!

Blue Ridge Community Theater  

Blue Ridge has a thriving art scene and our community theater has incredible local talent that will blow you away! Be sure to check out their calendar for the latest showings.

Huck’s General Store  

A trip to Blue Ridge would not be complete without a stop in Huck’s located on Main Street in Downtown Blue Ridge. Offering something for everyone, young and old, you will enjoy checking out the old fashioned candies, toys, games, books, puzzles, T-shirts. The best Blue Ridge souvenirs can be found here. Browse the aisles offering novelty handmade soaps, lotions, and more from local apothecaries. Check out the homemade salsas, kitchen goods, and so much more. A fun place to hang out waiting for the rain to stop!

Activities Surrounding Blue Ridge, GA

Tank Town  

This one is for our adrenaline junkies! Imagine being on a tank, in the rain, running over cars and old machinery. Does this sound fun to you? Well look no further than Tank Town USA! This rain-or-shine facility operates just 6-miles away from Downtown Blue Ridge and offers a ton of fun for both family and friends who just want a little adrenaline in the Blue Ridge area! Plus it’s more fun in the rain ~

Mercier Orchards  

Mercier Orchards is a family and veteran owned apple orchard settled in the Blue Ridge mountains. Offering amazing views, incredible food and delicacies, a winery, and more, this is a must-visit on any trip to the Blue Ridge area. Even during the rain, Merciers can entertain even the largest of gatherings!

Planning a cabin trip to Blue Ridge, GA can be a fun experience whether it rains or shines. With so many indoor activities to keep everyone occupied, you can be sure the rain won't dampen your experience in Blue Ridge!
title: Things To Do on a Rainy Weekend
